Amethyst Wedding Ring with Dinosaur Bone, Meteorite and Purple Wood

$6,849 – $7,079

Price varies by ring size. Cannot be resized after production.

Materials

Gibeon Meteorite14k White GoldDinosaur Bone
Ring Sizing Guide

Not sure of your size? A sizer set is included with your purchase, or order a custom made ring sizer for $10. Be sure to select the same width as the ring you want — a wide ring fits tighter than a thin ring in the same size.

Pricing by Size

$6,849 Sizes 5–13
$7,079 Sizes 5–13
Order on Jewelry by Johan Ask a Question
Checkout, customization, and payment are completed securely on jewelrybyjohan.com
✍ Free laser engraving
✓ 30-day returns
★ Handmade in USA
◆ Authentic meteorite

Rare materials come together in a stunning display in this amethyst wedding ring for men. The purple box elder burl between the dinosaur bone and Gibeon meteorite pairs perfectly with the breathtaking amethyst.

Crushed vs. Solid Dinosaur Bone Opt for solid if you'd like a more prominent pattern and bolder color. Crushed dinosaur bone is coated with Ring Armor protection (includes lifetime warranty), usually has a darker color, more subtle pattern and costs less.

RING LAYOUT
Ring Width: 10.25 mm
Ring Sleeve: 14k White Gold
Ring Profile: Flat
Ring Finish: Polished

1 mm 14k White Gold
3 mm Dinosaur Bone
0.5 mm 14k White Gold
1 mm Purple Box Elder Burl Wood
0.75 mm 14k White Gold
3 mm Split Gibeon Meteorite
1 mm 14k White Gold

Amethyst (Qty: 1)
Shape/Size: Princess Cut, 3 mm x 3 mm
Quality: Genuine, AA
Setting: Channel Setting

Behind the Piece

This piece is cut and set entirely by hand by Johan in Minneapolis, USA. The Gibeon meteorite inlay is etched with acid to reveal its unique Widmanstätten crystal pattern — a formation that took millions of years at approximately 1°C per million years of cooling.

No two rings will ever share the same pattern. Yours is singular, permanent, and genuinely cosmic.

Product Type
Rings
Vendor
Jewelry By Johan
Materials
Gibeon Meteorite, 14k White Gold, Dinosaur Bone
Available Sizes
Custom — select at checkout
Price Range
$6,849 – $7,079
Origin
Handmade in USA
Meteorite Source
Gibeon, Namibia
Meteorite Age
~4.5 billion years
💧

Avoid Prolonged Moisture

Brief water exposure (hand-washing, light rain) is fine. Remove before swimming in saltwater or chlorinated pools.

🧴

No Harsh Chemicals

Keep away from cleaning products, bleach, solvents, and acids. Remove your ring before household cleaning.

✨

Monthly Cleaning

Rinse under cool water, dry immediately with a soft cloth. Apply a drop of mineral oil or Renaissance Wax monthly.

🔄

Re-Etching Available

Over years of wear, the Widmanstätten pattern may soften. Send it back to Johan for a fresh acid etch to restore its full drama.

📦

Storage

Store in a dry environment. A silica gel packet in your jewelry box will absorb ambient moisture.

🔧

Lifetime Support

Johan stands behind every piece. Contact us any time for structural issues, re-etching, Ring Armor repair, or warranty questions.

Read Full Care Guide →

Production & Shipping

Production time 2–4 weeks (handmade to order)
US shipping Free on orders over $500
International Available — rates calculated at checkout
Packaging Premium gift box, certificate of authenticity
Insurance All shipments fully insured

Services & Warranty

Laser engraving Free — up to 25 characters, choice of fonts
Meteorite protection Free lifetime cleaning & re-etching — you pay shipping to us
Ring Armor repair Free lifetime repair of antler, wood & crushed material sealing
Cannot be resized Our meteorite inlay rings cannot be resized after production — size carefully
FAQ — engraving, sizing & more → RGA Requests & Returns →

Return Policy

Return window 30 days from delivery — in-store credit issued
Condition Normal wear considered based on time owned
Wrong size ordered 50% remake fee; original ring returned within 30 days required
Custom ring sizer If sizer fit and ring arrives wrong size, we remake at no cost (one time)
Cancel window 3 business days from order confirmation for full refund
After cancel window 50% refund as in-store credit
Engraving defects Covered under return policy if error is ours
Warranty void if Ring taken to another jeweler for any alteration

Our policy reflects the custom, handmade nature of every piece. We are not responsible for acts of God, misuse, or abuse. We highly recommend adding your ring to your homeowner's insurance (typically 1–2% of value annually) or insuring through Jeweler's Mutual.
